hyperedges.label: Test Selection Audit Tool release bundle

hyperedges.evidence: The Markdown front matter and quick-start define install path, MenuConfig merge target, mount point, and manifest-backed installation contract.
Controller methods share selected actors, label reads, label writes, and UI status/list refreshes across find/replace and batch rename operations.
The manual publish README lists immutability, required exports, ZIP sha256 consistency, and install-method visibility as acceptance requirements.

hyperedges.label: CLI installation pipeline
hyperedges.description: The CLI workflow searches, shows, plans, downloads, dry-runs, installs, merges MenuConfig, creates an install ledger, and creates a MenuConfig backup.

hyperedges.label: Manual ZIP installation pipeline
hyperedges.description: The manual ZIP workflow verifies checksum, inspects/unzips the package, validates manifest terms, copies files, edits MenuConfig, validates JSON, and snapshots the result.

hyperedges.label: ActorRenameController runtime behavior
hyperedges.description: The controller binds Chameleon UI data, reads selected actors, displays labels/tags, performs find/replace renames, and performs padded sequential batch renames.

hyperedges.evidence: Plan and install share version resolution, path checks, MenuConfig diffing, file planning, and install adds package verification plus ledger write.
Manual zip install artifacts collectively record package contents, successful CLI verification, valid MenuConfig, final installed files, and installed file hashes.
Unit tests cover hash comparison, ZIP entry extraction, path containment, MenuConfig merge planning, and ledger lifecycle used by CLI install/verify/uninstall.

hyperedges.relation: generates validated API records and downloadable artifacts from JSON and markdown tool sources
guards install workflows against overwrite loss, hash mismatch, cross-domain download, path traversal, duplicate menu entries, and malformed packages
keeps runtime validation schemas and exported TypeScript contracts aligned for tools, manifests, and API responses

The Markdown controller section references ActorRenameTool/ActorRenameTool.py, which defines ActorRenameController.
Front matter and architecture summary list mountPoint: OnToolBarChameleon.
installPath is <Project>/TA/TAPython/Python/ActorRenameTool/ and quick-start instructs copying ActorRenameTool/ under TA/TAPython/Python.
Quick-start and menuConfigMerge target instruct merging MenuConfig.snippet.json into <Project>/TA/TAPython/UI/MenuConfig.json.
ActorRenameController defines __init__(self, json_path).
ActorRenameController defines _get_selected_actors(self).
ActorRenameController defines refresh_actor_list(self).
ActorRenameController defines execute_find_replace(self).
ActorRenameController defines execute_batch_rename(self).
__init__ assigns self.data = unreal.PythonBPLib.get_chameleon_data(json_path).
__init__ stores the Chameleon data object on self.data for subsequent get_text, get_is_checked, and set_text operations.
__init__ calls self.data.set_text("txt_status", "就绪。请在场景中选中目标 Actor。").
_get_selected_actors calls unreal.EditorLevelLibrary.get_selected_level_actors().
refresh_actor_list starts with actors = self._get_selected_actors().
execute_find_replace iterates for actor in self._get_selected_actors().
execute_batch_rename assigns actors = self._get_selected_actors().
refresh_actor_list appends actor.get_actor_label() to the selected actor listing.
refresh_actor_list writes either '(未选中任何 Actor)' or the joined actor lines to txt_actor_list.
refresh_actor_list writes selection status and count messages to txt_status.
execute_find_replace calls self.refresh_actor_list() after updating status.
execute_batch_rename calls self.refresh_actor_list() after writing status.
execute_find_replace reads self.data.get_text("input_find").
execute_find_replace reads self.data.get_text("input_replace") or an empty string.
execute_find_replace reads self.data.get_is_checked("chk_case_sensitive").
execute_find_replace sets label = actor.get_actor_label().
execute_find_replace calls actor.set_actor_label(new_label) when the label changes.
execute_batch_rename reads self.data.get_text("input_base_name").
execute_batch_rename reads self.data.get_text("input_start_index") with default 1.
execute_batch_rename reads self.data.get_text("input_padding") with a minimum padding of 1.
execute_batch_rename calls actor.set_actor_label with base_name, incremented index, and zero padding.
CLI help lists hub ping, search, show, plan, download, and install commands requiring --hub <url>; hub ping log confirms http://127.0.0.1:8787 is reachable.
The CLI publish source file list includes source-data/actor-rename-tool.md.
The CLI publish generated file list includes generated-data/README.md.
The CLI publish generated file list includes generated-data/tool.md.
The manual publish README says no new submission was made because the approved version 1.2.0 is already published and same slug/version must be blocked.
Manual publish acceptance requires manifest.json export, and generated-release-file-list.txt includes generated-release-files/manifest.json.
Manual publish generated artifact table and file list include actor-rename-tool-1.2.0.zip.
Generated README content mirrors the source Markdown usage, file list, architecture, MenuConfig, View, Controller, and Agent installation sections.
Generated tool.md content mirrors the source Markdown usage, file list, architecture, MenuConfig, View, Controller, and Agent installation sections.
package-sha256.txt records e5d531e07a00b5b6b1ed74fdbffd675d9998090aa8b2f1747d91db543648feb8 for actor-rename-tool-1.2.0.zip.

links.description: The experiment demonstrates a full CLI installation of Actor Rename Tool.
The experiment manually installs Actor Rename Tool from a ZIP package.
The tool detail output lists <Project>/TA/TAPython/Python/ActorRenameTool/ as the install path.
The show output and install plans identify OnToolBarChameleon as the mount point.
The package SHA256 shown in CLI details matches the manual ZIP experiment checksum.
Tool metadata advertises Tag add/clear features, but the Python files in this chunk only show tag preview behavior.
The temporary project starts with TA/TAPython/Python and TA/TAPython/UI/MenuConfig.json.
The download log records manifest.json and actor-rename-tool-1.2.0.zip downloaded to the experiment downloads folder.
The CLI download step stores manifest.json alongside the ZIP.
The dry-run output reports Package hash valid.
The install output reports one MenuConfig item added.
The README and final file listing include tmp-project/TA/TAPython/.tool-hub/installed.json.
The final file list includes a timestamped MenuConfig.json.bak file.
Both experiments install Actor Rename Tool 1.2.0 with the same ZIP checksum and converge on the same TAPython files, but differ in automation and audit outputs.
The manual flow starts with shasum -a 256 and records the expected ZIP sha256.
The manual flow unzips actor-rename-tool-1.2.0.zip into extracted/.
The manual process reads extracted/manifest.json to confirm installPath, file list, MenuConfig target, and item to add.
The after-copy file list shows ActorRenameTool files under the temporary project's TAPython Python directory.
The manual flow edits MenuConfig.json to add the Actor Rename Tool entry and validates JSON.
The README's comparison table states that CLI generates the install ledger automatically while manual ZIP install does not by default.
The comparison table says backups are automatic in CLI installs but require manual preparation for ZIP installs.
The manual validation checklist says manifest.installPath must be <Project>/TA/TAPython/Python/ActorRenameTool/.
The final MenuConfig item uses ExtensionHookName OnToolBarChameleon.
The manual validation checklist says manifest.menuConfigMerge.target must be <Project>/TA/TAPython/UI/MenuConfig.json.
The extracted README lists MenuConfig.snippet.json in the package file inventory.
The extracted package documentation references ActorRenameTool/ActorRenameTool.py as the controller file.
The controller constructor stores get_chameleon_data(json_path) in self.data and initializes txt_status.
_get_selected_actors returns the current editor selected level actors as a list.
All listed __init__.py files are empty while the controller logic lives in ActorRenameTool.py.
The code displays tags in refresh_actor_list but the listed controller methods in this chunk do not modify tags despite package docs mentioning tag management.
refresh_actor_list calls _get_selected_actors before rendering the selected Actor list.
execute_find_replace loops over actors returned by _get_selected_actors.
execute_batch_rename loops over the current selected actors.
refresh_actor_list appends each actor's get_actor_label() value to txt_actor_list.
execute_find_replace reads the old label, performs case-sensitive or case-insensitive replacement, and calls set_actor_label when it changes.
execute_batch_rename sets labels as base_name plus a zero-padded incrementing index.
refresh_actor_list converts actor.tags to strings and includes them beside labels when present.
execute_find_replace refreshes the UI list after applying label changes.
execute_batch_rename refreshes the UI list after applying labels.

links.evidence: COMMANDS maps search/show/plan/download/verify/install/uninstall/doctor to run handlers.
hub ping branch builds context and awaits runPing(ctx).
global parseArgs includes json boolean and buildContext stores json.
install: runInstall in COMMANDS.
verify: runVerify in COMMANDS.
plan: runPlan in COMMANDS.
download: runDownload in COMMANDS.
uninstall: runUninstall in COMMANDS.
doctor: runDoctor in COMMANDS.
run builds `${hub.replace(/\/$/, '')}/health` and fetches JSON.
search fetches /api/tools/index.json then filters by slug/name/description/category/tags/author.
show fetches /api/tools/${slug}.json and prints metadata/downloads.
plan fetches tool detail by slug from hub.
plan calls resolveToolVersion(data.tool, requestedVersion).
plan constructs InstallPlan with tool, downloads, checks, filePlan, menuConfigDiff, warnings, next command/step.
plan reads MenuConfig and calls planMenuConfigMerge to compute itemsToAdd.
both commands resolve version, expand install paths, compute filePlan, compute MenuConfig diff, and emit InstallPlan-compatible data.
download fetches tool detail by slug before selecting version downloads.
download computes actualSha256 and validates packageSha256 when present.
download writes manifest.json and ZIP package files that verify accepts through --manifest and --package.
verify reads package file and calls readZipEntries(packageBuffer).
verify hashes each ZIP entry content and compares to manifest file sha256.
verify output says all files verified successfully for four manifest files.
slug install path fetches `${hub}/api/tools/${target}.json`; URL install path fetches manifest directly.
slug install path calls resolveToolVersion(data.tool, requestedVersion).
install constructs InstallPlan before dry-run or confirmation.
install calls planMenuConfigMerge before writing files.
install verifies packageSha256 before write and each manifest file hash before extraction.
install calls readZipEntries(packageBuffer) before validating and writing manifest files.
install reads ledger, adds tool record, and writes ledger after successful file/MenuConfig writes.
catch block awaits rollback(writtenFiles) before rethrowing install failure.
doctor checks hub reachability, TAPython directory, and MenuConfig writability, matching install prerequisites.
uninstall finds installed tool, removes files/menu items, removes tool from ledger, and writes ledger.
json without --yes returns action, filesToRemove, menuItemsToRemove, installDir, and backup path.
doctor fetches `${hubBase}/health` and records Hub check.
doctor calls detectTAPythonDir(absProject) and adds TAPython directory check.
doctor builds TA/TAPython/UI/MenuConfig.json and calls checkFileWritable.
InstallPlan includes filePlan entries with add/overwrite/skip actions.
rollback iterates writtenFiles in reverse, restoring backups or removing newly written targets.
tests expandInstallPath project placeholder and resolveInside traversal rejection.
final files are under tmp-project/TA/TAPython/Python/ActorRenameTool and TA/TAPython/UI/MenuConfig.json.
menuConfigDiff target is expanded from manifest.menuConfigMerge.target.
MenuConfig JSON valid.
tests adding, skipping duplicate, missing mount point, and mixed MenuConfig items.
tests sha256Buffer known values and verifySha256 match/mismatch/length behavior.
actor-rename-tool-1.2.0.zip SHA256 is e5d531e07a00b5b6b1ed74fdbffd675d9998090aa8b2f1747d91db543648feb8.
per-installed-file hashes correspond to the same files verified by CLI verify.
tests ZIP entry reading, manifest SHA256 matching, and invalid ZIP rejection.
ZIP list shows 7 files: ActorRenameTool files, manifest.json, MenuConfig.snippet.json, README.md, tool.md.
tests read/write/add/replace/find/remove ledger behavior.
installed-file-sha256 lists hashes for MenuConfig.snippet.json, ActorRenameTool.json, ActorRenameTool.py, and empty __init__.py.
Copies an existing target to a timestamped .bak file before replacement workflows can proceed.
sha256File reads file content and returns sha256Buffer(content).
Uses timingSafeEqual over hex buffers and rejects unequal lengths or invalid hex.
request parses the URL protocol and calls getTransport(parsed.protocol).
fetchJson awaits request(url) before status checking and JSON parsing.
fetchBuffer awaits request(url) before status checking and returning body.
Throws CliError for request timeout and excessive redirects.
Throws CliError for non-2xx responses and invalid JSON bodies.
Throws CliError for non-2xx responses.
Rejects download URLs whose hostname differs from the hub hostname unless an override is used by caller.
Throws CROSS_DOMAIN_DOWNLOAD CliError for mismatched hostnames.
Returns parsed InstallLedger or default { schemaVersion: '1.0.0', tools: [] } on ENOENT.
Replaces an existing tool with the same slug or pushes the new record.
Finds a slug index and splices the matching installed tool record from ledger.tools.
readLedger obtains ledgerPath by calling getLedgerPath(projectRoot).
writeLedger obtains ledgerPath by calling getLedgerPath(projectRoot).
Partitions requested menu items into itemsToAdd and itemsSkipped and returns MenuConfigDiff metadata.
Uses diff.mountPoint and diff.itemsToAdd to append menu config entries.
Formats target, mountPoint, itemsToAdd, and itemsSkipped into human-readable lines.
applyMenuConfigMerge begins by awaiting readMenuConfig(filePath).
Avoids duplicate menu entries by comparing existing ChameleonTools values.
When mode is json, output delegates to printJson(jsonData).
When mode is not json, output delegates to printHuman(humanLines).
printTable emits header, divider, and row strings through printHuman.
InstallPlan file and tool paths can be based on installPath templates that expand <Project> to projectRoot.
Throws when a resolved relative path would escape the allowed base directory.
Returns whether <projectRoot>/TA/TAPython exists, matching InstallPlan.checks.tapythonDirExists semantics.
Returns writable/existence state matching InstallPlan.checks.menuConfigWritable behavior.
Throws CliError for invalid central directory signature and unsupported compression method.
Throws ZIP_CORRUPT CliError when the End of Central Directory signature is absent.
readZipEntries starts by calling findEocd(buffer).
index.ts exports all schemas from './schemas.js'.
The schema fields match the ToolManifest interface fields, including menuConfigMerge and file manifest structure.
The schema fields match the ToolRecord interface, including downloads, summary, documentationMarkdown, and versions.
toolIndexRecordSchema is created by picking fields from toolRecordSchema and extending with latestVersion/apiUrl.
toolDetailResponseSchema wraps a tool field with toolRecordSchema.
Calls validatePayload(toolIndexResponseSchema, index, 'tool index API') before writing index.json.
writeToolApi validates payload with toolDetailResponseSchema before writing the API JSON.
Returns manifest fields matching ToolManifest: schemaVersion, name, version, files, menuConfigMerge, checks, and timestamps.
Extracted asset path, sha256, and size become manifest file entries after stripAssetContent/buildManifest.
index.ts exports types from './types.js'.
generateToolData iterates over await readJsonTools(options.toolDataRoot).
generateToolData iterates over await readMarkdownTools(options).
generateToolData calls normalizeTool(tool) for each tool before writing outputs.
generateToolData awaits writeDownloads(tool, options.downloadRoot).
generateToolData awaits writeToolApi(tool, options.apiRoot).
index.ts exports generateToolData and its option/result types.
scripts/generate-data.mjs invokes generateToolData with data, API, and download roots.
Each parsed markdown document is converted with buildToolFromMarkdown(parsed.data, parsed.content, sourcePath, options.root).
buildToolFromMarkdown awaits extractCodeAssets(markdown, path.dirname(sourcePath)).
Builds latestManifest and previous version manifests through buildManifest(...).
writeDownloads writes an archive when package files exist by awaiting writePackageArchive(...).
Writes manifest.json, README.md, optional tool.md/assets, and package metadata for each version.
When a code fence references @file, extractCodeAssets reads fs.readFile(resolveInside(baseDir, referencedPath)).
writeAssetFiles resolves each asset path inside versionRoot before writing.
writePackageArchive writes fs.writeFile(archivePath, createZipArchive(entries, releasedAt)).
Builds a stored ZIP archive from local file headers, central directory entries, and EOCD record.
The tooling resolveInside implementation mirrors the CLI helper by rejecting resolved paths outside the base directory.
The script sets repository-relative roots and logs the number of generated tool API records.

Controller methods share selected actors, label reads, label writes, and UI status/list refreshes across find/replace and batch rename operations.
